statement: No game assets shipped or modified in place
Client must be fully playable offline
Only the originating player can sign an event
Event identity is its BLAKE3 CID; equality is bytewise
Inbox and outbox DAGs are append-only
Consumers apply events in topological order consistent with causes[]
No wall-clock synchronization required
Projections are pure functions of the event DAG
No shared mutable state; state is derived from events
Global invariants are soft with Lagrangian penalties
Divergence is a reported metric, not a failure
System never forces a rollback to reconcile state
MCP servers register at session start; mid-session .mcp.json changes require restart
ket_store returns stable content_cid but fresh node_cid each call; lineage equality must use content_cid
First-time k-stack builds fetch git deps + crates.io; CI must allow outbound HTTPS
Dev cloud sandbox resets between sessions; .ket persists only via git-ignored local dir or explicit export
External MCP servers may drop mid-session; tool availability is not static
Cannot ship any DD2 textures, meshes, audio, scripts, or decompiled code
Hooking DD2 is practical only via REFramework/Lua; direct memory patching is brittle across patches
Capcom patches rotate struct layouts; offset-based hooks need a versioned signature table
REFramework Lua runs on the render thread; heavy I/O must be off-thread
DD2 ships without kernel anti-cheat as of this writing; can change in a patch
Never write to the player's DD2 save file; mod state lives in a sidecar
DD2 Workshop is Capcom-moderated; network-code mods likely disallowed. Distribute via GitHub + Nexus
Mod license is permissive (MIT-compatible); no EULA overlay, no telemetry without opt-in
A relay storing player-authored content has DMCA exposure; keep relays dumb, CID-only, TTL-bounded
Player-identifying payloads cross GDPR; treat player IDs as pseudonymous keys
Most players behind symmetric NAT; pure P2P requires relay fallback; start relay-only
Players play offline for days; inbox must tolerate arbitrary drain delay
Bandwidth budget per session should be <1 MB/hour baseline for tethered players
One $5/mo VPS fans out ~10k CIDs/day; beyond that needs sharding or BYO-relay
Cannot replicate DD2 physics server-side; projections are best-effort
Assume one maintainer; anything needing 24/7 ops is out of scope
MVP runs on one donated VPS; scale-out is a later problem
No crash reports, metrics, or pings without explicit player opt-in

pattern: MP mods clients a mature SP hook loader; never rewrite it
1-2 obsessive maintainers carry first 2-3 years; 5+ teams die at first patch
Ship presence first, state sync later if ever
Steam GNS / Epic EOS for NAT traversal; volunteer infra burns out maintainers
Persistent mods treat local divergence as expected
Mod lives or dies by publisher posture; Capcom posture is title-dependent
Host-game patch cadence is the clock on mod viability

claim: LukeYui's Elden Ring Seamless Co-op (May 2022+) is closest structural precedent: P2P Steam networking, 6 players, removes fog walls, single maintainer, FromSoft tolerated
evidence: Nexus page + PC Gamer coverage confirm: P2P via Steam newer networking API, 5+host players, continuous open-world connection

claim: REFramework is actively maintained for DD2 at v1.5.x as of April 2026
evidence: DD2 REFramework Nexus page + SourceForge mirror v1.5.7 DD2.zip; 2 years post-launch sustained maintenance

claim: REFramework Lua scripts can require native DLLs; this is our networking escape hatch
evidence: REFramework docs confirm Lua scripting API with file I/O and native DLL loading; no native socket API documented, must bring own

claim: Distribution norm is Fluffy Mod Manager + PAK files; our bundle must match
evidence: Fluffy Mod Manager is top mod on DD2 Nexus; standard content format is PAK copied to manager dir

claim: DD2 Nexus hosts 1000+ mods; community velocity sustained 2yr post-launch
evidence: Nexus mods listing; UDD2P updated 2026-04-03; recent vocation overhauls and bestiary mods

claim: No dedicated DD2 multiplayer mod currently exists; green field
evidence: Search across Nexus top mods + REFramework mod listings + Steam community returns no MP mod for DD2

claim: DD2 shipped without kernel anticheat but Capcom has retrofitted EAC to other titles; treat as live risk
evidence: DD2 launch was EAC-free per community reporting; Capcom's MHW/MHR added EAC over time post-launch

claim: DD2 pawn system already has a Capcom-hosted cross-player sharing mechanism; potential hook surface or reference architecture
evidence: Pawn system ships as networked feature in DD2 base game; protocol details not publicly documented

title: DD2 patch cadence 2024-2026
why: patch cadence is the mod-viability clock per pat.version-brittleness-kills

title: Capcom's posture toward RE-Engine mods
why: publisher tolerance is load-bearing per pat.publisher-tolerance-is-load-bearing

title: DD2 active player base and mod velocity
why: relay economics scale with active community size

title: Steam GNS license + usability for third-party mod
why: determines if we can use Valve's NAT traversal infra

title: Is DD2 pawn network protocol publicly reverse-engineered?
why: deciding whether to piggyback or build parallel

title: REFramework Lua to native DLL ABI specifics
why: our networking escape hatch needs a working plugin path

title: DD2 online/offline boundary
why: what does DD2 already require online; social contract for mod

title: DD Online private server revival - Capcom posture
why: closest prior art where community RE'd a Capcom netcode and survived

title: Smallest REFramework plugin running GNS client off-thread

claim: DD2 patch cadence slowed to ~annual: 2.01 Sep 2024, Jan 2025, Jan 2026; quiet since. Lower clock on offset-break risk
evidence: SteamDB tracks Jan 16 2026 update; GameRant patch notes coverage confirms Ver 2.01 Sept 17 2024 + Jan 29 2025; Pupjunkies notes 2026 silence

claim: Capcom ran an RE-Engine-wide DMCA campaign in 2023 targeting YouTube videos showing any mod (DMC/RE/MHR). Not limited to paywalled mods
evidence: Power Up Gaming + ResetEra thread document Capcom Japan legal team takedown campaign Dec 2023 across RE Engine titles

claim: Capcom 2017 DMCA'd SF5 costume modders BrutalAce and Khaledantar666 over paywalled mods using third-party IP
evidence: PC Gamer, TechRaptor, TechSpot 2017 coverage confirm Patreon-based paywalled mods were DMCA target

claim: Steam GameNetworkingSockets is BSD-3-Clause, Steam not required; open-sourced 2018, provides P2P NAT traversal + reliable UDP
evidence: Valve GitHub repo confirms BSD-3 license; GameDeveloper + HN coverage confirm non-Steam usage path

claim: No public reverse-engineering of DD2 pawn network protocol exists. DD1 Dark Arisen had no public server emulator either, with community noting C&D risk from Capcom
evidence: Search across Steam communities, GitHub, Nexus returns no DD2 pawn protocol RE project; Dark Arisen community thread explicitly notes Capcom C&D risk deterred server emulation

claim: DD2 requires online for first launch (Denuvo activation); offline after that is supported but disables pawn rental (cross-player feature)
evidence: Beebom + Dualshockers guides confirm: Denuvo online check on first launch, subsequent offline OK, pawn rental loses cross-player feature when offline

claim: Players already accept 'some cross-player features require online' as a base-game contract. Our mod can ride that affordance
evidence: Steam community threads confirm users understand pawn rental needs online; no friction expected for equivalent mod affordance

claim: REFramework Lua require DLL path has env quirks; use reframework_plugin_initialize plugin ABI rather than plain Lua loadlib
evidence: GitHub issue #623 documents 'error loading module' for standard Lua require; docs reference plugin_initialize entrypoint for native plugins

claim: RE Engine community reverses assets, scripts, structs via REFramework; no public netcode reverse engineering work across RE Engine titles
evidence: REFramework targets RE2/RE3/RE7/REV/DMC5/MHR/DD2 for gameplay modding; search finds no public network protocol RE projects

claim: Dragon's Dogma Dark Arisen had multiple community coop mod attempts (including Nucleus-based) but all stalled; consensus was 'technically possible, decades to ship'
evidence: Steam DA community threads document attempts involving Nucleus + Cheat Engine scripts to control pawns; all reported as stalled

claim: Dragon's Dogma Online (2015-2019 JP MMO) has community private server revival efforts; closer prior art than Elden Ring for RE'd Capcom netcode
evidence: OPSC (Original Pawn Sharing Club) at opsc.dark-arisen.com documents community interest in preserving Dragon's Dogma networking across eras

statement: Capcom actively DMCAs RE-Engine mods (2017 SF5 paywall, 2023 YouTube-wide RE Engine campaign). Posture is hostile, not title-dependent
Mod visibility via YouTube/streaming is a DMCA trigger surface; minimize promotional video content, favor text + screenshots
Do not reverse-engineer Capcom's pawn network protocol; build parallel event bus using only Lua/IL2CPP surface reads
Zero public distribution is an acceptable floor. Friends-only via git clone.
No Nexus, no Fluffy bundle, no GitHub releases until ADR-0006 is superseded
No YouTube, no streaming, no blog posts about the mod
